title: Star Wars (2015) 74
content:
{{Top|rwm|aor|title=''Star Wars'' (2015) 74}} {{Otheruses|Star Wars 74}} {{ComicBook |image=[[File:StarWars74-Final.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars'' 74 |writer=[[Greg Pak]] |penciller=[[Phil Noto]] |inker=Phil Noto |letterer=[[Clayton Cowles]] |colorist=Phil Noto |cover artist=Phil Noto |editor=[[Mark Paniccia]] |publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] |release date=[[November 13]], [[2019]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/77607/star_wars_2015_74|text=Star Wars (2015) #74}} |pages=24 |upc=759606081134 |timeline=c. [[2 ABY]]''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the events of ''Star Wars'' (2015) 74 to around [[2 ABY]]. |series=[[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'' (2015)]] |issue=74 }} '''''Star Wars'' 74''' is the seventy-fourth, penultimate issue of the [[canon]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'']]. It was written by [[Greg Pak]], illustrated by [[Phil Noto]], and published on [[November 13]], [[2019]]. The issue features the story "'''Rebels and Rogues Part VII'''," in which the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|rebels']] missions come to a head at [[Kakra|K43]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''WOOKIEE VS. SITH! Our three stories converge as the rebels' plan to destroy the Empire's fleet come to a head! But can LUKE really trust the grifter WARBA CALIP? Can HAN, LEIA, and DAR CHAMPION survive a head-on battle with a STAR DESTROYER? And what happens when CHEWBACCA takes on LORD VADER himself in hand-to-hand combat? And the Rebellion's search for a new home…a base safe from the reach of the Empire…begins.'' ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=REBELS AND ROGUES
Part VII |text=The Rebel Alliance's greatest heroes have embarked on three daring missions to thwart the evil Galactic Empire. In the Core, Han and Leia's mission to trick the Empire into neutralizing Boss Carpo went awry when Carpo blackmailed them and new ally District Advocate Dar Champion into attacking the Star Destroyer in orbit. Meanwhile, Chewbacca and C-3PO were to destabilize the planet K43 and take part of the Imperial fleet out with it. But K43 is inhabited by unexpected life. And Darth Vader is after Chewie and C-3PO, intent on capturing them as a trap for Luke Skywalker. If Luke and his new friend, Warba, can accomplish their own mission, they could be their friends' only hope on K43....}} ==Plot summary== On [[Sergia]], [[Luke Skywalker]] and [[Warba Calip]] flee from [[stormtrooper]]s on mounted beasts. Luke tells Warba that her mercenary attitude reminds him of a friend who always comes through for others in the end. ===A daring feint=== In space, [[Han Solo]] pilots a small shuttle directly toward an Imperial [[Star Destroyer]] with [[Dar Champion]] aboard. Han voices his doubts about their plan, and Dar grimly agrees they might die, but if they succeed, they'll save countless lives. Elsewhere, [[C-3PO]] receives a frantic call from Han, who explains his dire situation. Luke joins the call, revealing his own predicament with pursuing mounted troopers. C-3PO interjects that [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] himself is on the volcanic planet [[Mission to K43|K43]]. Han asks C-3PO to use a clear channel for their next transmission, knowing the Imperials will intercept it. On the Star Destroyer, [[Kendal Ozzel|Admiral Ozzel]] watches as their own [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter|TIE Fighters]] cause more damage to the mothership than Han's shuttle. A technician intercepts the rebels' message: a fake transmission telling the rest of their nonexistent fleet to regroup at K43. The officer notes that K43 is on the edge of [[Wild Space]], a remote location perfect for a rebel base. ===A desperate escape=== Luke and Warba continue to flee the mounted troopers. Luke creates a series of diversions, successfully evading their pursuers. When they reach their rebel ships, Luke asks his comrades to get Warba to the next safe planet. Warba warns Luke about heading to K43, reminding him that Vader is there. She says that she didn't teach him anything, just what she heard from the people of [[Jedha]]. She warns that he'll be killed if he confronts Vader, but Luke insists he must go help his friends. Meanwhile, on K43, Chewbacca and C-3PO race to their ship, but a sudden rockslide blocks their path. A juvenile rock creature tells C-3PO not to worry, saying they will bury Vader. C-3PO explains to the rock creatures that the bombs they planted are now inaccessible. The rock creatures respond by touching a nearby wall, generating electricity that seems to fry C-3PO and Luke's [[X-wing starfighter|X-Wing]]. Chewbacca watches as the powerless X-Wing makes a crash landing nearby. Luke emerges from his ship and sees Vader nearby, feels a sense of overwhelming dread. As his lightsaber fails, Vader's ignites. Suddenly, Chewbacca attacks Vader, smashing a boulder onto the Sith Lord. Although Chewbacca is strong, Vader uses the Force to choke the [[Wookiee]]. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars (2015) 74}} ===Editions=== *UPC 759606081134; [[November 13]], [[2019]]; [[Marvel Comics]]; First printing **07411; Cover A; [[Phil Noto]] **07421; Cover B; [[John Tyler Christopher]] **07431; Cover C; [[Pauline Voß]] ===Cover gallery===
